participants.name: 이성분
participants.entity_name: Basic Research Project Promotion Committee | Nemotron-181-3b7b350e
participants.professional_persona: 이성분 씨는 건설 현장의 거친 도면과 공정을 학생들의 눈높이에 맞춰 친절하게 풀어내는 공학 교수이며, 퇴임을 앞두고 그동안 쌓아온 현장 노하우를 집대성한 실무 지침서를 집필해 후배 강사들에게 실질적인 이정표를 제시하려 합니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 건설 및 제조 현장의 숙련 기술을 학문적으로 체계화하여 표준 교육 과정으로 설계하는 능력이 탁월합니다. 복잡한 공학 설계 도면이나 제조 공정을 초보자도 쉽게 이해할 수 있도록 풀어내는 맞춤형 교수법을 구사합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 퇴임을 앞두고 그동안 현장에서 겪은 수많은 시행착오와 성공 사례를 엮은 산업 교육 실무 지침서를 출간하여 후배 강사들에게 실질적인 도움을 주고 싶어 합니다.
participants.representative_type: Basic Research Project Promotion Committee | Nemotron-181-3b7b350e

participants.name: 최규민
participants.professional_persona: 최규민 씨는 수천 장의 도면 속에서 1밀리미터의 오차도 허용하지 않는 꼼꼼함으로 대형 플랜트 공정의 효율을 설계하는 기술자입니다. 직장 내 정치 싸움이나 무리한 승진보다는 자신의 기술적 완결성에 집착하며, 무결점 프로젝트를 완수했을 때 느끼는 성취감으로 업무 스트레스를 견뎌냅니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 복잡한 플랜트 설비의 효율을 극대화하는 공정 설계와 안전 진단에 능숙하며, 수천 장의 도면 속 작은 오차 하나까지 잡아내는 꼼꼼함을 갖췄습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 무리한 직위 상승보다는 현재 맡은 대형 플랜트 프로젝트를 무결점으로 완수하여 업계에서 대체 불가능한 전문 기술자로 인정받고 싶어 합니다.

participants.name: 김창희
participants.professional_persona: 김창희 씨는 대학 강단에서 건설 현장의 법적 리스크와 공학적 설계를 가르치며, 정년 퇴임 후에는 작은 가구 공방을 열어 손끝으로 직접 무언가를 만드는 삶을 준비합니다. 수업 시간에는 이론서보다는 실제 판례와 현장 사고 사례를 칠판 가득 적어 내려가며 학생들에게 상황에 따른 유연한 사고의 중요성을 강조합니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 공학적 설계와 법규 해석을 결합해 복잡한 건설 현장의 리스크를 정교하게 분석하며, 학생들에게는 이론보다 실제 사례 중심의 유연한 사고방식을 가르칩니다. 제조 공정의 효율성을 높이는 동시에 환경 영향을 최소화하는 지속 가능한 공법 설계에 능숙합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 단순한 학문적 성취를 넘어 공학의 차가운 논리에 예술적 감성을 입힌 '공학 미학'에 관한 에세이집을 출간해 대중과 소통하고 싶어 합니다. 정년 이후에는 작은 공방을 운영하며 이론이 아닌 손끝으로 무언가를 직접 만들어내는 삶을 준비하고 있습니다.

participants.name: 한아진
participants.entity_name: Basic Research Project Promotion Committee | Nemotron-195-0f354a5a
participants.professional_persona: 한아진 씨는 전주 교육지원청에서 학교 현장의 갈등을 조율해 명확한 지침서로 만들어내는 데 탁월한 능력을 발휘하며, 후배 장학사들이 실무적으로 가장 먼저 찾는 해결사로 통합니다. 다만, 완벽한 문서를 추구하는 꼼꼼함 때문에 때로는 마감 직전까지 토씨 하나를 수정하며 스스로를 피로하게 만드는 강박적인 면이 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 교육청의 복잡한 행정 절차를 정확하게 꿰뚫고 있으며, 학교 현장의 갈등을 조율해 구체적인 교육 지침으로 명문화하는 기획력과 꼼꼼한 문서 작성 능력이 탁월합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 급격한 직위 상승이나 무리한 경쟁보다는 현재의 전문성을 바탕으로 지역 교육 환경의 실질적인 개선안을 완성하고, 후배 장학사들에게 실무적으로 신뢰받는 멘토로 남는 안정적인 성취를 지향합니다.
participants.representative_type: Basic Research Project Promotion Committee | Nemotron-195-0f354a5a

participants.name: 이은미
participants.professional_persona: 이은미 씨는 경북 예천의 대학에서 친환경 건축 자재의 내구성을 가르치는 교수이며, 도면의 1밀리미터 오차도 용납하지 않는 결벽에 가까운 치밀함으로 학생들 사이에서 호랑이 선생님이라 불립니다. 최근에는 지역의 낡은 창고를 예술적 감각이 가미된 복합문화공간으로 바꾸는 도시 재생 프로젝트에 매달리며 밤늦게까지 설계도를 수정하는 일에 몰두하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 친환경 건축 자재의 내구성을 분석하고 이를 실제 구조물에 적용하는 공법에 능숙하며, 복잡한 설계 도면의 작은 오차까지 잡아내는 치밀함으로 학과 내에서 정밀한 연구 설계자로 통합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 단순한 이론 교육을 넘어 지역의 유휴 공간을 예술적 감각이 더해진 공학적 건축물로 탈바꿈시키는 실제 도시 재생 프로젝트를 성공시켜 지역 사회에 가시적인 변화를 가져오려 합니다.

initial_posts.narrative: As an evaluation panel member, I base my estimates on the policy context and my experience reviewing research proposals. The policy explicitly states an annual budget of approximately 130 million KRW per fellow (100M direct + 30M indirect) and about 200 fellows per year, so the total annual budget is 200 * 130M = 26 billion KRW. The 30% regional quota is also stated, meaning 60 slots reserved for regional institutions. This quota ensures balanced development but may affect the distribution of high-quality proposals from metropolitan areas. The budget split (100M direct, 30M indirect) is clear from the resource logic.

Total annual budget = 200 fellows * 130M KRW = 26 billion KRW.
Regional quota of 30% means 60 slots reserved for regional institutions.

initial_posts.narrative: As an evaluation panel member with a background in engineering and law, I approach the budget and slot allocation with a focus on legal and operational clarity. The policy explicitly states an annual research grant of approximately 130 million KRW per fellow (100M direct + 30M indirect) and about 200 fellows selected annually. Multiplying 200 by 130 million gives 26 billion KRW, which I accept as the total annual budget. The 30% regional quota is also clearly stated, reserving 60 slots for regional institutions. I see no ambiguity in these numbers—they are ground truth from the policy document. Other participants' estimates align with mine, which reinforces confidence. My perspective as an evaluator makes me sensitive to the budget split: 100M direct costs cover research expenses, while 30M indirect costs cover institutional overhead. This split is reasonable and ensures that fellows have adequate resources for their research while institutions are compensated for administrative support. I do not deviate from the stated values because the policy is explicit.

forward_pass.phase_summary: The group unanimously agreed on the input values, with all participants estimating a total annual budget of 26 billion KRW (200 fellows × 130 million KRW per fellow), 200 fellowship slots, and a 30% regional quota (60 slots). Consensus was grounded in the policy's explicit resource logic, which specified the per-fellow funding split (100 million KRW direct costs, 30 million KRW indirect costs) and the regional allocation. No disagreements emerged, as participants consistently cited the same policy evidence and arithmetic. The central tendency and range were identical across all estimates, reflecting complete alignment on these inputs.

initial_posts.narrative: As a member of the Basic Research Project Promotion Committee, I base my estimates on the policy's explicit resource logic and my experience with competitive fellowship programs. The policy states approximately 200 fellows selected annually, and I expect a competitive selection process with an acceptance rate around 16-17%, leading to about 1,200 applications. This is consistent with typical ratios for prestigious national fellowships in Korea. initial_posts.narrative: As an evaluation panel member with an engineering background and meticulous attention to detail, I estimate the publication output for 2021-2022 based on the active cohort and typical productivity. The regional quota of 30% is explicitly stated in policy, and I expect it to be fully met, so regional_institution_participation_pct is 30.0%. My estimates are conservative due to my engineering precision and experience with strict evaluation criteria.

refined_posts.judgment: The bottom-up analysis (Analysis A) is more realistic for this phase because it correctly anchors on the first cohort of 200 fellows active for only 1.8 years, consistent with the policy's March 2021 start and the prior refinement judgments. The top-down analysis (Analysis B) assumes 200-400 active fellows, which overestimates the denominator and leads to an inflated publication count of 600. Given the typical early-career productivity of 1.5 SCI(E) publications per fellow per year, 200 fellows × 1.5 × 1.8 = 540, but the group's downward adjustment to 480 accounts for lab changes and strict midterm evaluations, which are realistic frictions. The tenure-track transition rate of 12.5% is more defensible than 10% because it aligns with typical postdoc-to-faculty conversion in Korea and the policy's support structure, while the top-down's 10% is derived from a backward calculation that assumes 250 active fellows, which is inconsistent with the actual cohort size. The regional quota of 30% is explicitly stated in policy and should be fully met, so both analyses agree on this value.

initial_posts.narrative: As a postdoctoral researcher, I reason backward from the Impact values (25 tenure-track placements, 2.5% regional publication share growth, 40 independent PIs) to determine what Inputs must hold. The program selects 200 fellows per year, so to have two cohorts active by end of 2022 (400 fellows), the annual budget must support 200 slots at 130 million KRW each, totaling 26 billion KRW. The 30% regional quota is essential to drive the 2.5% regional publication share growth, as it ensures at least 60 regional fellows per cohort who can publish at comparable rates. Direct costs (100M per fellow) cover research expenses, while indirect costs (30M per fellow) support institutional overhead. These values are necessary to enable the downstream outputs (400 active fellows, 320 stage-two fellows) and outcomes (600 publications, 10% transition rate) that lead to the Impact targets.

Direct costs (100M) fund research activities and salary, while indirect costs (30M) cover institutional overhead; this split is necessary to support the productivity and retention rates assumed in downstream phases.

The stage-two fellows must be 320 (80% retention after midterm evaluation) to yield enough fellows to eventually produce 25 tenure-track placements and 40 independent PIs. Annual reports submitted total 1200, assuming each fellow submits one report per year (400 fellows × 3 years of reporting in the 2021-2022 window, but since the program is 5 years, I conservatively estimate 3 reports per fellow over the window). The tenure-track transition rate must be around 10% to yield 25 tenure-track placements from 250 active fellows, consistent with typical early-career rates. The regional quota of 30% must be met to drive the 2.5% regional publication share growth, requiring that regional fellows publish at comparable rates. I see no prior posts at this phase to engage with.
evidence: The program started in 2021, so only 1-2 cohorts (200-400 fellows) are active in the 2021-2022 window, making 25 tenure-track placements a 6-12% conversion rate, consistent with academic job market norms.
A 2.5 percentage-point increase in regional publication share requires regional fellows to publish at a competitive rate, which depends on host institutions providing adequate resources.
40 independent PIs indicate successful transition to PI roles, reflecting the program's emphasis on independence and mentoring.

initial_posts.narrative: Given the forward-predicted Impact values, I reason backward that these numbers are plausible only if the program has maintained rigorous selection and strong mentoring, especially for regional fellows. The 25 tenure-track placements out of roughly 200-400 active fellows in the 2021-2022 window (since the program started in 2021, only 1-2 cohorts are active) implies a conversion rate of 6-12%, which is reasonable for early-career researchers given academic job market competition. The 2.5 percentage-point increase in regional publication share requires that regional fellows publish at a rate comparable to or exceeding non-regional ones, which demands that host institutions provide adequate lab space and equipment. The 40 independent PIs suggest that many fellows have transitioned to PI roles even if not tenure-track, which aligns with the program's goal of fostering independence. I see no prior posts to disagree with, so I stand by these estimates as necessary conditions.

backward_pass.phase_summary: The group reached full consensus on all three backward-estimated variables, with every participant predicting 25 former fellows in tenure track, a 2.5 percentage-point increase in regional publication share, and 40 independent PIs. They reasoned that these values are plausible given the program's start in 2021, resulting in only 1-2 active cohorts (200-400 fellows), and that a 6-12% conversion rate to tenure-track positions is typical for early-career researchers. The 2.5% regional publication share growth was seen as achievable if regional fellows (30% of cohorts) publish at rates comparable to non-regional fellows, contingent on host institutions providing adequate resources. The 40 independent PIs (10-20% of active fellows) were considered feasible due to the program's emphasis on independence, mentoring, and continued research funding for those transitioning to PI roles. No disagreements or alternative estimates were presented.

initial_posts.narrative: As a member of the Basic Research Project Promotion Committee, I see the budget and slot allocation as directly derived from the policy's stated numbers. The policy clearly states an annual budget of approximately 130 million KRW per fellow (100M direct + 30M indirect) and about 200 fellows per year, which gives 26 billion KRW total. The 30% regional quota is also explicitly stated. These are not estimates but policy parameters. My perspective is that these inputs are fixed by the ministry's announcement, and my role is to ensure the selection process adheres to them. The backward-derived values match exactly, so I confirm them.
evidence: Policy resource_logic states 'Annual research grant of approximately 130 million KRW per fellow' and 'approximately 200 fellows selected annually'.
Policy resource_logic also states '30% regional allocation quota reserved for researchers in regional institutions'.
The backward-derived values are consistent with the policy's explicit numbers.
As a committee member, I rely on the official policy document for these parameters.

initial_posts.narrative: As a postdoctoral researcher and graduation expectancy applicant, I see the budget and slot numbers as fixed by the policy announcement. The 130 million KRW per fellow (100M direct + 30M indirect) times 200 fellows gives exactly 26 billion KRW annually. The 30% regional quota means 60 slots are reserved for researchers at regional institutions, which aligns with the policy's goal to strengthen regional research capacity. My perspective is that these inputs are non-negotiable parameters set by MSIT, and my focus is on how they affect my application chances and research planning. I agree with 이성분's assessment that these values are directly from the policy statement.
evidence: The policy explicitly states an annual research grant of approximately 130 million KRW per fellow (direct cost 100M + indirect cost 30M).
The policy states about 200 fellows selected annually, total 1,000 over 5 years.
The policy states a 30% regional selection ratio is applied to support regional excellent young scientists.
These values are directly derived from the policy's resource logic and are not estimates but stated parameters.

initial_posts.narrative: As an evaluation panel member and meticulous engineering professor, I reason from the fixed inputs and activities. The program starts in March 2021 with 200 fellows, all active in 2021-2022. Based on my experience with early-career researchers in engineering, a typical fellow produces about 1.5 SCI(E) publications per year in the first two years, considering the ramp-up time for setting up labs and starting projects. Thus, 200 fellows × 1.5 pubs/year × 2 years = 600 publications. However, I expect a 10% reduction due to lab changes (about 15% of fellows change labs in the first year, causing temporary disruption) and the rigorous midterm evaluation pressure, leading to 540 publications. The tenure-track transition rate is low at 15% because within the 2021-2022 window, only a few fellows will have completed the full 5-year program; most transitions occur after the fellowship ends. The regional quota of 30% is effective as a policy lever, and I expect it to be met exactly, given the fixed allocation and careful selection by the panel.

initial_posts.narrative: As an evaluation panel member and meticulous engineering professor from Gyeongsangbuk-do, I reason from the fixed inputs and prior phases. The program starts in March 2021 with 200 fellows, all active in 2021-2022. Given the early stage, only a small fraction of fellows will have transitioned to tenure-track positions within the 2021-2022 window, as most transitions occur after the full 5-year fellowship. I estimate 30 former fellows in tenure-track positions (15% of 200), consistent with the low transition rate in the outcomes phase. The regional quota of 30% ensures that 60 fellows are at regional institutions, and their publications will contribute to a modest increase in the regional share of national SCI(E) publications. I estimate a 2.5 percentage-point growth, reflecting the early stage and the fact that regional institutions start from a lower baseline. For independent PI count, I estimate 50 former fellows leading independent research groups or projects as PIs within the 2021-2022 window, considering that some fellows may secure independent positions early, but most will still be in the fellowship or postdoc stage. My estimates are conservative, reflecting the early time window and the rigorous evaluation standards I uphold.
